name: Kirkpatrick Bank
description_short: Kirkpatrick Bank is a privately owned bank serving Oklahoma, Colorado, and surrounding markets with checking, savings, mortgages, and business banking solutions.
description_long: Kirkpatrick Bank is a privately owned financial institution celebrating its 55th anniversary in 2025. The bank operates in Oklahoma, Colorado, and surrounding regional markets, positioning itself as a community-focused alternative to larger national banks. The company emphasizes personalized service and direct relationships with customers and business clients. The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and commercial banking products. Personal services include Simply Free Checking (with $50 minimum opening deposit and no monthly maintenance fee), savings accounts with competitive rates, mortgage financing (including conventional, FHA, VA, and investment property loans), and digital payment solutions through Zelle partnerships and newly launched digital wallets. For business customers, Kirkpatrick Bank provides comprehensive business banking solutions described as treating client businesses "like our own." The institution also offers online and mobile banking available 24/7. Kirkpatrick Bank distinguishes itself through a privately owned structure, regional focus, and emphasis on personalized lending solutions. The bank highlights its mortgage team's ability to deliver customized solutions for primary residences, vacation properties, and investment opportunities. Leadership changes, such as the recent appointment of Todd Glass as EVP Head of Lending for Oklahoma, suggest active expansion of lending capabilities. The bank also demonstrates proactive fraud awareness through educational materials and anti-fraud protocols. While Kirkpatrick Bank offers standard banking services with competitive features like no-fee checking accounts, the website lacks detailed information about interest rates, CD terms, business loan specifics, or comprehensive product comparisons. Regional limitation to Oklahoma and Colorado may restrict accessibility for consumers in other areas. The FAQs section appears incomplete with placeholder text rather than substantive answers. Overall, Kirkpatrick Bank appears to be a solid regional banking option for customers seeking personalized service and community-oriented banking in its service areas.
services: Simply Free Checking accounts with no maintenance fees
Conventional mortgage loans for primary residences
Investment property and second mortgage lending
pros: Simply Free Checking with no monthly maintenance fee and only $50 minimum opening deposit
Zelle integration for fast peer-to-peer money transfers
Personalized mortgage lending team offering VA, FHA, conventional, and investment property loans
Digital wallet option for secure on-the-go payments
Proactive fraud prevention education and customer protection protocols
Privately owned structure emphasizing personalized service over corporate standardization
cons: Geographic limitation to Oklahoma, Colorado, and surrounding markets only—not available nationwide
Website FAQ section contains incomplete placeholder text rather than actual customer answers
Limited transparency on mortgage rates, CD terms, and business loan APRs on publicly available website
No mention of CD accounts, money market accounts, or other savings product details
Minimal information about business lending terms, qualification requirements, or loan amounts
best_for: Oklahoma and Colorado residents seeking personalized community banking with no-fee checking
Small business owners wanting relationship-based lending rather than automated underwriting
Home buyers seeking customized mortgage solutions including VA and FHA options
Consumers prioritizing local bank ownership and regional financial institution support
diagnosis: Kirkpatrick Bank is best for Oklahoma and Colorado residents seeking personalized community banking with competitive checking products and customized mortgage solutions. The main caveat is geographic limitation to two states and incomplete online information, requiring direct contact for specific rates, terms, and business lending details.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
